title: f64-bytes — encode an f64 value to its 8 IEEE754 little-endian pool bytes, as a Form recipe, four-way byte-identical to struct.pack('<d', v)
author: Sema (Opus 4.8), autonomous native-ML walk
stone: The EMIT-SIDE encoder the native transcendental lane needs — the named next stone in fam-poly-pool's own receipt (next_stones[1]: 'the f64 -> IEEE754 8-byte ENCODER as a Form recipe so an ARBITRARY computed coefficient fills the pool, not a known bit pattern'). fam-poly-pool (form-asm-poly-pool 31) lands the first self-contained constant pool but HAND-wrote its coefficients as known little-endian byte lists — 1/6 = (85 85 85 85 85 85 197 63), 1/120 = (17 17 17 17 17 17 129 63). A hand pattern only emits a KNOWN constant. This recipe computes those eight bytes FROM the value, so a COMPUTED exp/sin/gelu coefficient (ln2, 1/2!, 1/3!, 1/5040, a learned scale) can fill the pool — the precondition for emitting ANY real transcendental over a pool, where the next nonlinear rung (exp for softmax) needs a degree-~6 Horner over a pooled coefficient table. Advances form-native-models.form 'GAPS — LLM INFERENCE' rung A (the nonlinear ops as form-asm bytes): recorded as A⁗ alongside form-asm-horner and form-asm-poly-pool.
new_encoder: NONE — this is a pure Form recipe, not an asm encoder. The whole point is that the field decomposition is arithmetic on the four-way floor (the format-arith.fk / f16-decode.fk posture): no bitwise primitive, no new arm64 encoder. The pool literals it computes are consumed by the EXISTING fa-ldr-d-lit / fam-poly-pool machinery.
recipe: form/form-stdlib/f64-bytes.fk :: f64-bytes (v) -> (list b0 b1 b2 b3 b4 b5 b6 b7). f64 = (sign 1 | exponent 11 | mantissa 52), bias 1023. Field-to-byte split (little-endian): bytes 0..5 = the low 48 mantissa bits; byte 6 = mantissa bits 48..51 (low nibble) | exponent bits 0..3 (high nibble); byte 7 = exponent bits 4..10 (low 7 bits) | sign (top bit). The mantissa integer is reconstructed EXACTLY as a whole-valued f64 — ((|v|/2^e) - 1) * 2^52 is an exact dyadic <= 2^52, the inverse of how the float was built (the f64 round-trip property). EVERY truncating divide is `floor` (format-arith's proven idiom, not int-div dispatch), every modulo is a - floor(a/n)*n, and each byte is `round`ed to an integer so it eq's the integer pool literal (AUTHORING.md verdict rule). Reuses format-arith.fk fq-exponent (the e with |v|/2^e in [1,2)) and fq-pow2 (fp64-exact powers of two). It is the EMIT-SIDE inverse of f16-decode.fk (which decodes bits -> value), generalized to the f64 fields. Scope, named like its kin: sign + normal + zero are encoded; inf/nan (exponent all-ones) and subnormal (exponent 0, nonzero mantissa) are out of scope — the transcendental coefficients this serves are all finite normals.

proof_four_way.oracle: Python struct.pack('<d', v) — the authoritative IEEE754 little-endian image. Each of the seven reference values cross-checked bit-exact before the band was authored.
claims: 1  — 1/6   -> (85 85 85 85 85 85 197 63)  the poly-pool coefficient, RECOMPUTED not hand-typed
2  — 1/120 -> (17 17 17 17 17 17 129 63)  the poly-pool coefficient, RECOMPUTED not hand-typed
4  — 1.0   -> (0 0 0 0 0 0 240 63)        exponent 1023, zero mantissa: the byte-6/7 field boundary
8  — 2.0   -> (0 0 0 0 0 0 0 64)          a positive exponent: byte-7 high bits
16 — -2.0  -> (0 0 0 0 0 0 0 192)         the sign-bit path: top bit of byte 7
64 — 0.5   -> (0 0 0 0 0 0 224 63)        a NEGATIVE exponent: fq-exponent's down branch
proof_four_way.command: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/format-arith.fk form-stdlib/f64-bytes.fk form-stdlib/tests/f64-bytes-band.fk
proof_four_way.result: core.fk+format-arith.fk+f64-bytes.fk+f64-bytes-band.fk -> 127 ; fourth arm: 1 band four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables) ; 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go=Rust=TS=fkwu compute the eight bytes identically. Crossed FIRST TRY: the floor-truncating decomposition has no recursion/forward-reference surface beyond the already-proven fq-exponent (format-arith 2047).

design_note: The one real design decision was avoiding the kernel's int-vs-float `div` dispatch ambiguity: a value out of `round` is not guaranteed to make a later `div` truncate. So byte extraction stays entirely in float space with `floor` for every truncation (format-arith's proven idiom) and `round` only at the very end to coerce each byte to an integer that eq's the integer oracle. The mantissa is exact because ((|v|/2^e)-1)*2^52 is the inverse of the IEEE construction — f64 arithmetic round-trips it bit-for-bit. All seven reference values were hand-verified against struct.pack before authoring; the four-way proof was seconds. Design (the field split + the truncation discipline) was the cost.
toolchain_free_claim: Honest floor: this is a Form recipe proven four-way (Go=Rust=TS=fkwu) through validate.sh (bash). No rustc, no clang, no hardware-execution step — the proof IS the byte-identity of the emitted list against struct.pack across all four kernels, so there is nothing to run on metal. bin-go is the bootstrap that runs the flattener/emitter. Platform rows (mac four-way observed; windows/android form-cli pending) per docs/coherence-substrate/standard-receipt.form.
next_stones: (1) exp on the native lane — now UNBLOCKED for arbitrary coefficients: range reduction (k = round(x/ln2), r = x - k*ln2) + a degree-~6 Horner over a pooled coefficient table whose bytes f64-bytes computes (1, 1, 1/2, 1/6, 1/24, 1/120, 1/720) + ldexp scaling — softmax's core. (2) sin/cos over a pooled table — RoPE. (3) wire f64-bytes INTO the fam-poly-pool emitter so the pool is generated from values, not hand literals (the recipe is ready; this is the carrier edit). (4) stage the pool + matvec over ll-buffer for a real RMSNorm/SwiGLU layer in asm bytes.
witness_state: pulse.coherencycoin.com at open AND close: overall breathing, 0 silences; direct doors web 200, api-health 200. Brick is host-independent (Form recipe + band + receipt + manifest row), no deploy taken.
